recordedIncidents.note: Police recorded spiking incidents, England and Wales. The Alcohol (Minimum Pricing) Act 2012 — spiking became a specific recordable offence in 2022.

injectionSpiking.note: Injection spiking (using needles rather than drinks) as percentage of all reported spiking incidents.

prosecutionRate.note: Percentage of recorded spiking incidents resulting in prosecution. Evidence challenges — toxicology window is typically 12-24 hours.

sources.name: Home Office
sources.dataset: Police recorded crime open data tables

sources.name: Office for National Statistics
sources.dataset: Crime in England and Wales: year ending March 2024

metadata.methodology: Recorded incidents from Home Office police recorded crime data. Spiking became a separately recorded crime category in 2022; earlier figures estimated from assault and poisoning categories. Injection spiking proportion from NPCC national data collection. Prosecution rate from CPS charging data matched to NPCC crime records.
knownIssues: Pre-2022 data is estimated — spiking was not a separate recordable category before the Spiking Prevention Act
Toxicological evidence fades rapidly, creating fundamental evidential challenges for prosecution
Expert estimate of 47,000 actual cases based on reporting rate surveys — highly uncertain
Injection spiking figures based on police classification which may be inconsistent across forces
